what_it_does: Defines acceptance criteria for the Graph-instrument layout and card redesign, split into function and form requirements, proof checks, dependencies, open Tim rulings, and a build order.
observations: Frames each criterion as a function/form pair, with BY USE browser verification at desktop, portrait mobile, and landscape mobile widths.
Declares gated foundations for graph data projection, type-to-colour DNA data, and motion token emission.
Specifies layout changes from radial placement to an even chipboard-style grid derived from graph axes.
Requires type colour to resolve from addressed data, with icon shape carrying type recognition and state hues kept separate from type hues.
Requires always-visible directional edges, source-type edge colour, arrowheads, reduced-motion handling, and explicit edge-validity debt.
Defines move-not-teleport interaction through keyed DOM reconciliation, camera easing, survivor identity checks, exit transitions, zoom composition, and resize handling.
Keeps the existing plain-English card and filter interaction as regression-guarded requirements.
Includes substrate/addressing requirements and a cross-repo seam for shared palette and icon dependencies.
Ends with product-face gates for visual identity, type distinguishability, state/type separation, opacity honesty, edge calm, gold restraint, single V presence, and responsive recompute.
standouts: The file treats visual acceptance as a testable product surface, not only an implementation checklist.
The move-not-teleport section is unusually concrete about DOM identity, camera continuity, exit phase, zoom composition, and resize interruption proofs.
The colour requirements explicitly separate type hues, state hues, focus gold, folder bronze, and ghost neutral treatment.
conventions: Uses numbered criterion IDs such as F1, L1, E1, M1, CD1, S1, and PF-1.
Marks status and verification semantics with bracket tags including [GATE], [PROOF], [OPEN→Tim], and BY USE.
Maintains explicit DEPENDS and BLOCKS relationships between criteria.
Names unresolved decisions as OPEN→Tim anchors while still describing buildable seams.
Uses function/form language throughout to pair mechanism checks with by-sight reading.
concerns: Several criteria depend on OPEN→Tim rulings, so this file intentionally cannot define a fully closed completion state by itself.
Some referenced OPEN-FOR-TIM items are cited but not included in this file body.
Embedded source line references may require maintenance as implementation files move.
notes: This is a hardened acceptance ledger for making the graph redesign data-derived, visually on-DNA, continuously animated, and browser-verifiable.
questions: Where is the OPEN-FOR-TIM list that resolves #G1, #G2, #G3, #G5, #G6, #G7, and #G8?
Which type-to-colour value-set shape did Tim choose: per-type hues or coarse families?
What exact content signal should satisfy FV1: satellites, content glyph, drill-only reveal, or another form?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it defines the acceptance bar for the graph layout and card redesign, with function/form criteria, proofs, dependencies, and unresolved rulings.
intention_signals: It seems intended to convert a visual redesign into an auditable implementation contract that preserves ConceptV identity while preventing hardcoded colours, teleporting redraws, invalid edges, and unverified responsive behavior.
novelty: Combines design-language acceptance, substrate data rules, DOM identity proofs, pixel-area measurement, and open decision seams in one completion ledger.
contribution: Contributes a detailed acceptance schema for a graph UI substrate, including data projection requirements, tokenized colour and motion mechanisms, layout and edge behavior, motion reconciliation proofs, card/filter regression guards, substrate re-entry checks, and product-face gates that a fused system would keep as verification material.
summary_for_embedding: A completion-criteria document for the counterpart-design graph redesign that specifies gated data, token, layout, colour, edge, motion, card, substrate, and product-face requirements with browser and proof checks.
intention_for_embedding: Written to make the graph redesign buildable and verifiable as an on-DNA, data-derived, continuously animated instrument while preserving clear unresolved decision seams.
